Article Title: Lionel Messi exits FIFA World Cup in tears and as football's greatest
Article Text:
Argentina's Lionel Messi (10) during the World Cup final match between Spain and Argentina in East Rutherford, N.J., near New York, Sunday, July 19, 2026. (AP Photo)

Tears rolled down Lionel Messi 's face as he walked away with a silver medal around his neck. Spain had beaten Argentina 1-0 after extra time in the FIFA World Cup final, ending the defending champions' title defence and what could well be the last World Cup appearance of the greatest footballer the game has seen.Twenty years after making his World Cup debut, the 39-year-old could not produce one more miracle. Argentina, chasing back-to-back World Cup titles, were outplayed by Spain for long periods at MetLife Stadium, where 80,663 spectators had largely turned up hoping to watch their Messi-ah lift the trophy once again.Instead, it was Spain celebrating their second World Cup title.Ferran Torres scored just 37 seconds into the second half of extra time to settle the final after Spain had dominated the contest from the opening whistle. As Spanish players celebrated, Messi stood with his teammates, struggling to hold back tears while Argentina's supporters applauded them from the stands.Messi, head bowed, hands on his hips, watched 19-year-old Lamine Yamal embrace Torres after the winning goal. Moments later, with tears running down his cheeks, he collected his runners-up medal while Spain celebrated amid confetti and fireworks."I hope that everyone feels proud of him, of what he’s achieved because he’s the best football player ever stepping foot on a pitch," Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni said after the match.The statistics reflected Spain's dominance in the final. Luis de la Fuente's side outshot Argentina 20-2 and completed 845 passes to Argentina's 433. According to Opta, the 18-shot difference was the second biggest in a World Cup final since records began in 1966, behind only West Germany's 23-1 shot advantage over Argentina in the 1990 final.Spain also succeeded where few teams have ever managed to -- they kept Messi quiet."The first idea was to keep Messi at bay," Spain coach Luis de la Fuente said.Messi managed only 15 touches in the first half and finished with 54 touches in the match. He never found the space to create the moments that had carried Argentina through the tournament. His passes into dangerous areas failed to connect and he rarely received the ball in positions from where he could threaten Spain's defence.That had not been the story of Argentina's campaign until the final.After leaving European football for Major League Soccer three years ago, some questioned whether Messi's best days were behind him. Instead, he led Argentina back to another World Cup final with eight goals and four assists.His eight goals helped Argentina through the group stage and knockout rounds. Against England in the semifinal, he set up both goals in a stoppage-time victory. Against Egypt, with Argentina trailing 2-0 and facing elimination, he provided the cross for the first goal before scoring the equaliser himself.Throughout the tournament, Messi once again showed his ability to influence matches despite covering less ground than his teammates. He walked for nearly half the time he spent on the pitch, averaging around five kilometres per game, conserving energy before producing bursts of quality whenever Argentina needed him.Against Spain, though, that moment never arrived.If this was his last, then Messi ends his World Cup career with 21 goals, a record that stood until France's Kylian Mbappe scored twice in the third-place playoff to move to 22. He also became the first player to start three World Cup finals and has 125 international goals, second only to Cristiano Ronaldo's 146.His achievements extend far beyond statistics.World Cup winner in 2022. Consecutive Copa América titles. Four UEFA Champions League trophies. Twelve league titles across spells with Barcelona and Paris Saint-Germain, only player in football history to win 8 Ballon d'Or awards. A career that placed him alongside Diego Maradona and Pelé in football's greatest-ever debates.As the final whistle blew, Spanish players queued up to console him. Several hugged him before Argentina collected their silver medals. When Spain formed a guard of honour for the runners-up, Messi tried to smile but could not. His expression barely changed as opponents patted him on the back while he walked to the podium.If this was indeed his final appearance on football's biggest stage, it ended without the fairytale finish many had hoped for.It was not because Messi failed to try. Spain simply gave him no room to operate. Their Rodri-led midfield controlled possession, limited Argentina's attacks and prevented Messi from dictating the game the way he had done throughout the tournament.The final may not have delivered the ending he wanted, but it does little to change how his career will be remembered.It may not have been the ending Messi imagined. It may not even be the end. Whether this proves to be Messi's final World Cup appearance or not will become clear only when he decides. But if this was the last image of him on football's biggest stage, it was one of tears, not triumph.However, the defeat in New Jersey cannot undo a career that has already secured its place among the greatest the sport has known. If this is to be his tearful sign off, fans will still look back at having been blessed by football's greatest of all time.
Article Summary: Spain beat Argentina 1-0 after extra time in the FIFA World Cup final. Ferran Torres scored just 37 seconds into the second half of extra time to settle the final. Argentina, chasing back-to-back World Cup titles, were outplayed by Spain for long periods.

Article Title: FIFA World Cup 2026 awards: Full winners list as Rodri, Mbappe claim top honours
Article Text:
L-R: Spain's Pau Cubarsi, Rodri and goalkeeper Unai Simon pose with their individual awards. (AP Photo)

Mbappe wins Golden Boot despite France's semifinal exit



Full list of FIFA World Cup 2026 award winners



Golden Ball: Rodri (Spain)

Silver Ball: Lionel Messi (Argentina)

Bronze Ball: Kylian Mbappe (France)

Golden Boot: Kylian Mbappe (France)

Silver Boot: Lionel Messi (Argentina)

Bronze Boot: Jude Bellingham (England)

Golden Glove: Unai Simon (Spain)

FIFA Young Player Award: Pau Cubarsi (Spain)

FIFA Fair Play Trophy: The Netherlands

Spain's historic FIFA World Cup 2026 triumph was reflected in the tournament's individual honours as Rodri, Unai Simon and Pau Cubarsi swept three of the biggest awards, while France captain Kylian Mbappe claimed the Golden Boot after another sensational goalscoring campaign.Following Spain's 1-0 extra-time victory over Argentina in the final at MetLife Stadium, FIFA announced the winners of its prestigious post-tournament awards, recognising the standout performers of the biggest World Cup in history.Rodri was named the recipient of the Golden Ball after producing a series of commanding displays in midfield throughout Spain's unbeaten title-winning run. The Manchester City midfielder dictated the tempo in almost every game and capped off a remarkable tournament by helping La Roja secure their second FIFA World Cup crown.Goalkeeper Unai Simon added another trophy to Spain's celebrations after winning the Golden Glove Award. Simon was the backbone of Spain's formidable defence, keeping seven clean sheets in eight matches as the champions conceded just one goal throughout the tournament. He also set a World Cup record by going 650 consecutive minutes without conceding.Teenage sensation Pau Cubarsi completed Spain's awards sweep by winning the FIFA Best Young Player Award.The 19-year-old defender emerged as one of the tournament's biggest stars with a string of composed performances at the heart of Spain's defence, playing a crucial role in the team's record-setting defensive campaign.France captain Kylian Mbappe once again finished as the tournament's leading scorer, collecting the Golden Boot after scoring 10 goals in eight matches.Although France fell short of reaching a third consecutive World Cup final, Mbappe's remarkable campaign saw him become the first player to win the World Cup Golden Boot in back-to-back editions. He also extended his record as the highest goalscorer in men's FIFA World Cup history with 22 career goals.Meanwhile, the Netherlands were presented with the FIFA Fair Play Award in recognition of their exemplary discipline and sportsmanship throughout the competition.Spain capped a memorable tournament by defeating defending champions Argentina 1-0 after extra time in the final, with substitute Ferran Torres scoring the decisive goal in the 106th minute. The victory secured Spain's second FIFA World Cup title and completed a near-perfect campaign, with La Roja conceding just one goal across eight matches while dominating both the team and individual honours.
Article Summary: FIFA announce the winners of its prestigious post-tournament awards. Spain's historic FIFA World Cup 2026 triumph was reflected in the tournament's individual honours. Rodri, Unai Simon and Pau Cubarsi swept three of the biggest awards. France captain Kylian Mbappe claimed the Golden Boot after another sensational goalscoring campaign.

Article Title: NEET-UG results declared: 2 score 715/720, 19 cross 700
Article Text: NEW DELHI: NEET-UG 2026 saw a sharp rise in top scores after the paper-leak controversy and nationwide retest, with Punjab’s Aryan Gupta topping the exam with a 715 score. Haryana’s Panshul Bansal who also scored 715 out of 720 is the second topper based on a tie-breaker rule. Their score was 29 marks higher than the 686 secured by the 2025 topper, when an unusually tough paper ensured that not a single candidate crossed 700.The results also threw up a strong first-attempt and gender story. More than 93% of the 138 candidates scoring above 690 were appearing for NEET for the first time, while 99% were aged 17 to 19. Women accounted for over 58% of the 11.2 lakh candidates who qualified and recorded a higher success rate — 57% of women who appeared cleared the test, compared with 55% of men.The National Testing Agency declared the results Thursday night after close to 20 lakh candidates took the June 21 re-examination at 5,440 centres in 551 Indian cities and 14 cities abroad. The original examination, held on May 3, was cancelled on May 12 following allegations that the question paper had been compromised. The CBI is investigating the leak.The rise in top scores was striking. Nineteen candidates scored above 700.A total of 1,492 candidates secured 650 or more, 10,160 crossed 600 and 90,780 scored at least 500. The 138 candidates scoring above 690 came from 66 cities.The number of qualifiers declined from 12.36 lakh in 2025 to 11.2 lakh this year, but the number appearing also fell from 22 lakh to close to 20 lakh. The overall qualification rate, therefore, remained broadly stable at around 56%, despite the cancellation and re-test. Uttar Pradesh again produced the largest pool of successful candidates, with more than 1.7 lakh qualifying, while Lakshadweep had 43. The top 17 candidates this year, all scoring above 705, came from eight states — Punjab, Haryana, Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh, Maharashtra, Bihar, Tamil Nadu and Telangana. Seventeen state toppers scored 700 or more and 26 crossed 690. State and UT toppers included Ladakh’s Jigmet Yangchan Lamo with 530, Andaman and Nicobar Islands’ Dhruv Tripathi with 606 and Lakshadweep’s Fahmida Anees with 573. Every northeastern state was also represented in the state-topper list.Though NTA has not released a rural-urban or school-background classification, the presence of the 138 highest scorers across 66 cities, along with state toppers from remote UTs and every northeastern state, indicates a wide geographical spread beyond the largest metropolitan centres.Among social categories, 5.12 lakh OBC-NCL candidates qualified, followed by 2.91 lakh general-category candidates, 1.6 lakh SC candidates, 95,026 from Gen-EWS and 63,716 from ST communities. The successful pool also included 3,666 PwBD and 303 PwD candidates. NEET was conducted in 13 languages.NTA said the result was declared in time to keep medical counselling and admissions on track. Candidates will now enter counselling for MBBS, BDS, AYUSH and allied programmes. Following the paper-leak controversy, govt has decided to shift NEET-UG from the pen-and-paper format to a computer-based test from 2027.
Article Summary: Punjab’s Aryan Gupta topped the exam with a 715 score. The original examination, held on May 3, was cancelled on May 12 following allegations that the question paper had been compromised. Uttar Pradesh again produced the largest pool of successful candidates.

Article Title: Lionel Messi, Lamine Yamal share emotional hug after Spain's FIFA World Cup triumph - WATCH
Article Text:
Lionel Messi and Lamine Yamal (PTI Photo)

Torres scores title-winning goal



'It was a goal scored by 47 million people'



Spain complete dominant performance



NEW DELHI: Lionel Messi and Spain youngster Lamine Yamal shared a heartfelt hug after the final whistle of the FIFA World Cup 2026 final on Sunday, giving fans one of the most memorable moments of the tournament.After Spain beat defending champions Argentina 1-0 in extra time to win the title, Messi and Yamal met on the pitch, embraced each other and had a brief chat. The touching moment quickly went viral on social media.Their hug carried extra meaning because of their famous connection from the 2007 UNICEF photoshoot, when a young Messi was photographed with baby Yamal.At 39, Messi became only the second footballer to play in three FIFA World Cup finals, while 19-year-old Yamal celebrated winning the World Cup in his first final appearance.Spain secured their second FIFA World Cup title thanks to substitute Ferran Torres, who scored the only goal of the match in the 106th minute of extra time.The move began just after the restart of the second period of extra time. Argentina were already down to 10 men after Enzo Fernandez was sent off in the 93rd minute.Lamine Yamal collected the ball on the right wing and passed it to Pedro Porro. Porro then delivered a cross into the penalty area that was almost going out of play.Nico Williams, who had come on in the 75th minute, headed the ball back into the middle, where Torres reacted quickly and fired a left-footed shot past goalkeeper Emiliano Martínez.It was Spain's 20th shot of the match, while Argentina had still not managed a single attempt at goal.Torres said the winning goal belonged to the entire country and reflected on the criticism he had faced during the tournament.“I think at the end of the day, it was a goal scored by 47 million people,” Torres said. “I feel very happy. I was heavily criticized during the World Cup, but I think it was written in destiny and things. But I always have that chance, that option to continue, and God gives it to those who believe it, who deserve it most.”After scoring, the Barcelona forward celebrated by kicking down the corner flag before being surrounded by his teammates as Spain's fans erupted in celebration.Spain controlled the match for long periods and created chance after chance before finally breaking Argentina's resistance in extra time.Marc Cucurella started the move from the back by passing to Eric García, who found Martín Zubimendi. The midfielder then released Yamal on the right wing, leading to the move that eventually produced Torres' winner.Torres almost added a second goal later in the match but was ruled offside. Spain then defended Argentina's late corner kicks to seal a famous victory and lift the World Cup trophy for the second time.
Article Summary: Ferran Torres scored the only goal of the match in the 106th minute of extra time. Argentina were already down to 10 men after Enzo Fernandez was sent off in the 93rd minute. Messi and Yamal met on the pitch, embraced each other and had a brief chat.

Article Title: Revealed: The eye-watering amount Spain players will earn for winning the 2026 FIFA World Cup
Article Text:
Spain's Rodri (16) lifts the trophy as Spain celebrates after winning the World Cup final soccer match between Spain and Argentina in East Rutherford, N.J., near New York, Sunday, July 19, 2026. (AP Photo/Ashley Landis)

How much each Spain player will receive

Argentina receive runners-up prize

Argentina walks on the pitch after their loss in the World Cup final soccer match between Spain and Argentina in East Rutherford, N.J., near New York, Sunday, July 19, 2026. (AP Photo/Julio Cortez)

Record prize money across the tournament

Champions - Spain: $51 million

Runners-up - Argentina: $34 million

Third place - England: $30 million

Fourth place - France: $28 million

Quarter-finalists (5th-8th): Switzerland, Norway, Belgium, Morocco: $20 million each

Round of 16 (9th-16th): Colombia, Egypt, United States, Portugal, Mexico, Brazil, Paraguay and Canada: $16 million each

Last 32 (17th-32nd): Ghana, Algeria, Australia, Cape Verde, Austria, Croatia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Senegal, DR Congo, Ecuador, Sweden, Ivory Coast, Netherlands, Germany, Japan and South Africa: $12 million each

Group stage (33rd-48th): Uruguay, Scotland, South Korea, Iran, Uzbekistan, Iraq, Saudi Arabia, Curaçao, Czech Republic, Qatar, Panama, Jordan, Tunisia, New Zealand, Haiti and Turkey: $10 million each

Spain's triumph at the 2026 FIFA World Cup did not only bring the nation's second world title. It also secured one of the biggest financial rewards ever distributed to a World Cup-winning squad after FIFA increased the tournament's prize money to record levels.Luis de la Fuente's side defeated Argentina 1-0 after extra time in the final at the New York New Jersey Stadium, with Ferran Torres scoring the decisive goal in the 106th minute. The victory earned the Royal Spanish Football Federation (RFEF) the tournament's top prize of $51 million from FIFA.According to reports, Spain's players will each receive a substantial share of that prize under a pre-agreed bonus arrangement negotiated between the squad captains and the RFEF.FIFA awards federations a single payment once their World Cup campaign concludes, with the amount determined by their final finishing position rather than after each knockout round.By merely reaching the World Cup final, Spain had already guaranteed the RFEF $34 million.Under the agreement reported by AS, 40 per cent of that guaranteed prize money would have been allocated to the players. That would have created a dressing-room bonus pool worth approximately $13.6 million, equating to just over $523,000 for each member of Spain's 26-man squad before taxes.Winning the World Cup, however, significantly increased those figures.After lifting the trophy, Spain's players became entitled to 45 per cent of FIFA's winners' prize.That means approximately $22.95 million will be shared between the 26 players, with each squad member expected to receive around $882,700 before taxes, according to AS. The remainder of FIFA's prize money will stay with the RFEF, which will use part of the windfall to cover the costs of Spain's World Cup campaign while also benefiting financially from one of the most lucrative tournaments in its history.Although Argentina fell short of retaining the World Cup after Ferran Torres' extra-time winner, Lionel Scaloni's side will still receive a substantial runners-up payment.FIFA will award the Argentine Football Association $34 million for finishing second.Unlike Spain, however, no official details have yet emerged regarding how that prize money will be distributed among Argentina's 26-player squad.The 2026 World Cup featured the largest prize fund in the competition's history, with FIFA increasing overall financial rewards by around 50 per cent compared with previous editions.Prize money based on final finishing positions is as follows:Those performance-based payments account for $703 million of FIFA's total $871 million tournament prize fund.The remaining $168 million is distributed among participating national associations regardless of where they finish, ensuring every nation receives additional financial support for taking part in football's biggest tournament.For Spain, however, victory delivered far more than just the World Cup trophy. Alongside a place in football history, every member of the squad is set to receive one of the largest bonuses ever awarded to a Spanish national team, reflecting both FIFA's record prize fund and the agreement reached between the players and the RFEF.
Article Summary: The 2026 World Cup featured the largest prize fund in the competition's history, with FIFA increasing overall financial rewards by around 50 per cent compared with previous editions. Spain's players will each receive a substantial share of that prize under a pre-agreed bonus arrangement negotiated between the squad captains and the RFEF.

Article Title: 20 shots to none: How Spain utterly owned Argentina in FIFA World Cup 2026 final and rendered Lionel Messi irrelevant
Article Text: For years, Spanish football has been synonymous with control - of the ball, of space, of tempo. On Sunday night at New York New Jersey Stadium, Spain produced perhaps the ultimate expression of that philosophy, reducing defending champions Argentina and Lionel Messi to mere passengers in the FIFA World Cup 2026 final.Ferran Torres' 106th-minute winner secured a 1-0 victory after extra time and delivered Spain's second FIFA World Cup title. But the scoreline barely hinted at the gulf between the two sides. This was not simply a victory. It was a footballing masterclass that left Argentina chasing shadows for almost two hours.The numbers told the story as emphatically as the result. Spain unleashed 20 shots, 12 of them on target, completed 738 of their 817 passes and forced Emiliano Martínez into one save after another. Argentina, by contrast, failed to muster a single shot in regular time.That extraordinary statistic places the 2026 final in unique company. According to Opta, among nearly 900 World Cup matches dating back to 1966, only three teams have failed to attempt a shot during normal time: Costa Rica against Brazil in 1990, Costa Rica against Spain in 2022, and now Argentina against Spain in the 2026 final. The first two were underdogs expected to survive.The third was the reigning world champion led by Messi.Spain ensured there was never a platform for Argentina to settle. Fabian Ruiz, later Pedri, dictated the rhythm from midfield, Alex Beana and Rodri constantly found pockets between the lines, while Dani Elmo and, later Nico Williams Jr, and Lamine Yamal stretched the pitch with relentless width. Argentina spent most of the night retreating, reorganising and defending.Messi, who had carried Argentina through another deep World Cup run at the age of 39, found himself isolated. Every touch was immediately met by red shirts, every passing lane closed, every attempt to slow the game and impose his rhythm suffocated almost before it began. It was one of the quietest nights of his World Cup career - not because he lacked ambition, but because Spain never allowed the contest to be played on his terms.If anything illustrated Spain's control, it was Argentina's inability to escape their own half. The South Americans finished the 90 minutes with just 34% possession - their lowest share of the ball in any World Cup match since Opta's records began in 1966.Even more revealing, goalkeeper Emiliano Martínez finished as player with most touches for Argentina, underlining how relentlessly Spain pinned the holders back. Spain did not merely outplay Argentina; they monopolised the ball, dictated the territory and deprived the champions of the rhythm that had defined their title defence.Only Martínez prevented the contest from ending much earlier. The Aston Villa goalkeeper produced a string of outstanding saves to deny Williams, Pedri, and Torres, repeatedly frustrating Spain as wave after wave of attacks crashed into Argentina's penalty area.Eventually, however, even Martínez could not withstand the pressure.Sixteen minutes into extra time, Williams managed to cushion a header into the path of Torres, who fired his finish beyond the goalkeeper. It was a goal Spain had threatened all evening and one that finally rewarded their dominance.Argentina's hopes had taken a major dent when Enzo Fernández was sent off in stoppage time, reducing Lionel Scaloni's side to 10 men and extinguishing any realistic prospect of an Argentine surge. Even before the red card, though, the balance of the contest had long been decided.Spain's triumph was built not on moments of brilliance alone but on collective superiority. Luis de la Fuente's side controlled possession, dictated territory and suffocated every avenue Argentina attempted to explore.Their pressing denied transitions, their midfield recycled possession with remarkable composure and their defensive line gave Messi and Julián Álvarez little room to breathe.For Argentina, the defeat marked the end of a reign that began in Qatar four years ago. Whether this ultimately proves to be Messi's final World Cup appearance remains to be seen, but if it is, it closed not with one last moment of genius but with Spain rendering football's greatest player almost anonymous.World Cup finals are often remembered for drama, controversy or moments of individual inspiration. This one will be remembered for something far rarer - complete domination.Spain didn't just beat the defending champions. They made them disappear.
Article Summary: Ferran Torres' 106th-minute winner secured a 1-0 victory after extra time and delivered Spain's second FIFA World Cup title. Spain unleashed 20 shots, 12 of them on target, completed 738 of their 817 passes and forced Emiliano Martínez into one save after another. Argentina failed to muster a single shot in regular time.

Article Title: 'Hurts me in the soul': Lionel Scaloni breaks down after Argentina's FIFA World Cup final loss - WATCH
Article Text:
Lionel Scaloni

Uncertain about his future



NEW DELHI: Argentina head coach Lionel Scaloni struggled to hold back tears after his side lost 0-1 to Spain in the FIFA World Cup 2026 final at the New York New Jersey Stadium on Sunday.Scaloni said the defeat was deeply painful as Argentina failed to defend the title they had won in Qatar four years ago. Ferran Torres scored the only goal in extra time to hand Spain their second World Cup crown.Speaking after the match, the Argentina coach said: "We had injuries in key positions that we weren't expecting. The wear and tear has been enormous, they gave their last effort. I'm in a dream place for everyone, I always thank the President (Tapia) for the opportunity. This place is wonderful. It's hard to form a group like this. Losing hurts me in the soul."Scaloni also raised doubts about whether he would continue as Argentina coach. His contract runs until December, and he said he would first speak with Argentina Football Association president Claudio Tapia before making any decision."I will talk to the president," he said. "I have an idea of what I would like to do. I will see out my contract and I feel the need, I don't know if something as big can be done.We need to speak this through."He thanked Tapia for giving him the opportunity to coach the national team and said he needed time to think."I am thankful to the president for bringing me this chance of being in this place where I am at the moment. It was a dream place for everyone. We have tried up until the very last minute to give our utmost, the staff and players. I believe it's only fair that I can take this time for myself to think this through.""Bear with me, I don't know if I can continue. This place is wonderful, it is a dream place."He explained that building another successful team would not be easy."We would never have imagined, including my staff, that we would be in this place. In order to continue you need a great deal of things, especially resetting your mind, rebooting, creating a group like this again. That is very difficult to create again."
Article Summary: Argentina lost 0-1 to Spain in the FIFA World Cup 2026 final at the New York New Jersey Stadium. Ferran Torres scored the only goal in extra time to hand Spain their second World Cup crown. Lionel Scaloni said the defeat was deeply painful as Argentina failed to defend the title.

Article Title: 'Let the noise be there': Rohit Sharma brushes aside retirement talk after brilliant Lord's century
Article Text:
Rohit Sharma (BCCI Photo)

Retirement rumours before Lord's ODI



BCCI clears the air



NEW DELHI: There was plenty of speculation before the third ODI against England at Lord's that Rohit Sharma could be playing his final one-day international for India. The veteran opener answered those rumours in his own style, smashing a superb 138 off 110 balls. His innings included 17 fours and five sixes as he almost guided India to a record chase.England had posted a massive 387/3, and despite Rohit's brilliant effort, India fell 27 runs short in the series finale.Speaking to BCCI.tv after the match, Rohit said he pays no attention to the outside noise and remains focused only on helping the team."Look, my job is with the bat. Come and play. Represent my country, represent the team. That's what I've been told to do since I made my debut. So that's what I'm gonna do," Rohit said."The noise... Since I made my debut, was there and till the time I'm gonna stay here, it's always gonna be there. So it doesn't really matter too much. What matters is what I do on the field. Try and contribute to the success of the team. That's all my focus is right now," the former India captain said."And let the noise be there. You know there's no noise. There's no fun.My job is inside, their job is outside, so that's how I look at it," Rohit added.Speculation had spread on social media before the Lord's match that it could be Rohit's final ODI in Indian colours.Instead, the 38-year-old reminded everyone of his class with one of his finest ODI knocks, keeping India in the chase until the closing stages.Earlier, The Times of India had reported that Rohit's place in India's ODI XI was no longer guaranteed, with the selectors planning to pick players based on form and fitness as preparations begin for the 2027 ODI World Cup. The report added that the door remains open for Rohit to continue, provided he keeps performing consistently.Rohit is currently just 12 matches away from becoming only the eighth player in ODI history to make 300 appearances. He has played 288 ODIs and scored 11895 runs at an average of 48.95, including 38 centuries and 62 fifties.Amid the speculation, BCCI Secretary Devajit Saikia dismissed reports that the Lord's ODI would be Rohit's last match for India."There are lot of speculations going on in media about Rohit Sharma's future. I want to firmly assert that there has been no such discussion that Rohit will be playing his last match at Lord's on Sunday," Saikia told PTI.He also made it clear that the veteran opener remains part of India's ODI plans."Rohit is a regular member of the Indian ODI team and he will continue to represent the country as long as he is in the scheme of things. In other words, Lord's ODI won't be his last match," the BCCI secretary confirmed.
Article Summary: Speculation had spread on social media before the Lord's match that it could be Rohit's final ODI in Indian colours. The 38-year-old smashed 138 off 110 balls, including 17 fours and five sixes. Despite his brilliant effort, India fell 27 runs short in the series finale. Rohit is just 12 matches away from becoming only the eighth player in ODI history to make 300 appearances.

Article Title: Awkward scenes! Donald Trump remains on podium during Spain's FIFA World Cup trophy celebrations; Gianni Infantino steps in - WATCH
Article Text:
PTI Photo

Boos greet Trump during trophy ceremony



Torres fires Spain to second World Cup title



Trump's earlier World Cup controversy



NEW DELHI: US President Donald Trump became one of the biggest talking points after Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 to win the FIFA World Cup 2026 final at New York New Jersey Stadium on Sunday.Trump stood alongside the Spain players as they received the World Cup trophy and remained on the podium during the celebrations.Trump only stepped aside after FIFA president Gianni Infantino pulled him away, allowing captain Rodri to lift the trophy with his teammates. The moment quickly went viral on social media.The US President also handed the FIFA World Cup trophy to the Spanish players after the final at New York New Jersey Stadium.Boos echoed around the stadium when Trump and Infantino walked onto the field for the trophy presentation after the match.Trump, who had arrived at the stadium by helicopter, congratulated players with handshakes as they walked across the stage before standing with Spain's squad during the trophy presentation.Spain beat defending champions Argentina 1-0 after extra time thanks to substitute Ferran Torres' goal in the 106th minute.Spain dominated possession and created far more chances throughout the match, while Lionel Messi 's Argentina managed to force extra time before finally conceding.Torres reacted quickest to a loose ball inside the box and fired a left-footed shot under the crossbar to seal Spain's second FIFA World Cup title, their first since 2010.Trump had earlier found himself at the centre of controversy during the tournament after calling FIFA president Gianni Infantino to review the red card shown to US striker Folarin Balogun in the group-stage match against Bosnia and Herzegovina.Balogun's one-match suspension was later lifted, allowing him to play in the Round of 16 against Belgium, where the United States lost 4-1.
Article Summary: Spain beat Argentina 1-0 after extra time to win the FIFA World Cup 2026 final at New York New Jersey Stadium on Sunday. Substitute Ferran Torres scored the only goal of the game in the 106th minute. US President Donald Trump stood alongside the Spain players as they received the World Cup trophy.

Article Title: BTS World Cup Halftime Show: Date, timing, venue and where to watch in the US, Canada, Mexico, India, Japan, and more
Article Text:
BTS is set to headline the historic FIFA World Cup 2026 Final Halftime Show, marking the tournament's first-ever halftime spectacle. The performance will take place during the July 19 final at New York New Jersey Stadium. Fans can watch it through official broadcasters worldwide, including Zee5 in India. The event also features Shakira, Madonna, Justin Bieber and others, while supporting the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund for children's education and sports initiatives.

Article Title: 'He's a disgrace': Argentina star sent off after grabbing Spain player's throat in ugly World Cup final scenes
Article Text:
Spain's Gavi, left, falls as he scuffles with Argentina's Leandro Paredes (5) and Thiago Almada during the World Cup final soccer match between Spain and Argentina in East Rutherford, N.J., near New York, Sunday, July 19, 2026. (AP Photo/Yuki Iwamura)

Tempers had already begun to rise during the match

Referee Slavko Vincic, of Slovenia, shows a red card to Argentina's Enzo Fernandez (24) during the World Cup final soccer match between Spain and Argentina in East Rutherford, N.J., near New York, Sunday, July 19, 2026. (AP Photo/Frank Franklin II)

Spain's celebrations became the flashpoint

Clash with Gavi leads to red card

Players scuffle et the end of the World Cup final soccer match between Spain and Argentina in East Rutherford, N.J., near New York, Sunday, July 19, 2026. (AP Photo/Matt Slocum)

Neville and Shearer condemn Argentina's reaction

Bitter ending to Argentina's title defence

Spain's historic FIFA World Cup triumph ended amid chaotic scenes after the final whistle, with Argentina midfielder Leandro Paredes shown a straight red card following an ugly confrontation involving several players from both teams.Luis de la Fuente's side defeated defending champions Argentina 1-0 after extra time at the New York New Jersey Stadium to lift the 2026 FIFA World Cup, with Ferran Torres scoring the decisive goal. But moments after Spain's celebrations began, tensions boiled over into a mass confrontation that resulted in Paredes being dismissed and left an otherwise memorable final overshadowed by controversy.Although the final produced just one goal across 120 minutes, the contest was fiercely contested throughout.Argentina had already been reduced to 10 men late in normal time when Enzo Fernández received his second yellow card after a reckless challenge on Spain defender Pau Cubarsí.Earlier in extra time, Lionel Messi also became involved in an exchange with referee Slavko Vinčić, arguing that Marc Cucurella should be punished after appearing to speak while covering his mouth during an on-field exchange.Those incidents set the tone for an increasingly heated conclusion before emotions spilled over completely once the final whistle confirmed Spain as world champions.As soon as Vinčić ended the match, Spain's substitutes sprinted from the bench onto the pitch to celebrate with their teammates.According to reports, Argentina defender Nahuel Molina appeared to swing an arm towards Spain captain Rodri as the midfielder ran past him during the celebrations.Rodri immediately stopped and confronted Molina, with both players exchanging words before Spain defender Eric García stepped between them in an attempt to calm the situation.Instead, the confrontation escalated.Paredes rushed towards the scene and allegedly grabbed García by the throat before shoving him as players from both teams converged around the incident.Argentina manager Lionel Scaloni quickly arrived and was seen attempting to separate players while gesturing for calm, but the confrontation continued to spread across the pitch.With one confrontation still unfolding, another quickly developed nearby.Spain midfielder Gavi had become entangled with Argentina's Thiago Almada when Paredes turned his attention towards the young Spaniard.Television footage appeared to show Paredes wrestling Gavi to the ground before grabbing him by his substitute's bib and pushing into his face.Several Spain players immediately rushed across to shield Gavi, while striker Borja Iglesias was among those who confronted the Argentina midfielder.Witnessing the sequence unfold, referee Vinčić had little hesitation.Paredes, who had already been booked during the match and had earlier escaped what many believed could have been a second caution, was shown a straight red card after the final whistle for his involvement in the altercation.The scenes drew immediate criticism from television pundits covering the final.Speaking on ITV, former England captain Gary Neville said: "He's a disgrace."I mean he was lucky not to be sent off during the game. I love the competitiveness of the Argentinians and they deserve great credit for what they've done in this tournament. Fighting and scrapping for their lives every game."Even today when they were outplayed by a great football side. Messi's propped them up at times and they're very competitive and I love that element of them."But he's just been a disgrace the last couple of games and why the referees have allowed him to continue to do what he's doing."BBC pundit Alan Shearer was equally critical after reviewing the footage."Paredes threw two or three haymakers into someone's face. He went for them," Shearer said."There is no place or space for that."We know how much it means to them and we know how much it hurts to lose, but there is a way to lose. Too many times we have seen that from Argentina. The reaction after the final whistle is terrible."The chaotic finale brought a disappointing conclusion to Argentina's attempt to retain the World Cup.Lionel Scaloni's side had fought through a demanding knockout campaign and reached a second consecutive World Cup final, but they were second best against a Spain side that dominated possession and eventually found the breakthrough in extra time through Ferran Torres.As Spain celebrated becoming world champions for a second time, Argentina's players were left to reflect not only on the defeat but also on an ill-tempered ending that culminated in Paredes' dismissal after the final whistle.For Lionel Messi, who was widely expected to be making his final World Cup appearance, the evening ended with visible emotion as he applauded Argentina's travelling supporters before collecting his runners-up medal, while the post-match disorder involving several teammates threatened to overshadow what had been another remarkable tournament for the defending champions.
Article Summary: Argentina midfielder Leandro Paredes shown a straight red card following an ugly confrontation involving several players from both teams. Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 after extra time at the New York New Jersey Stadium to lift the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Moments after Spain's celebrations began, tensions boiled over into a mass confrontation.

Article Title: Will Lionel Messi retire? Argentina coach breaks silence after FIFA World Cup 2026 final defeat
Article Text:
Lionel Messi (PTI Photo)

Spain end Argentina's reign



Torres and Yamal shine for Spain



Spain's complete team effort



NEW DELHI: Will Lionel Messi retire? Was the FIFA World Cup 2026 final his last appearance on football's biggest stage?Those questions dominated discussions as Argentina took on Spain in the final at the New York New Jersey Stadium. Argentina's title defence ended with a 1-0 extra-time defeat, but head coach Lionel Scaloni made it clear that Messi's future would be entirely his own decision.After the final, Scaloni praised the 39-year-old captain and said Messi would continue playing for as long as he wanted."Well, he's 39 years old now. Unbelievable. There's nothing new to discover from my side. It was crystal clear for me that he's going to play up until he decided to do so, and that all the team players, team-mates, and all of his people were going to support him. And I'm sure I hope that everyone feels proud of him, of what he's achieved, because he's the best football player ever," Scaloni said as quoted by The Athletic.Scaloni's comments came after Argentina fell short in their bid to defend the World Cup title they won in Qatar in 2022.Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 after extra time to lift their second FIFA World Cup title and their first since 2010.Substitute Ferran Torres scored the only goal of the match in the 106th minute, beating Emiliano Martinez with a powerful finish after a move involving Lamine Yamal, Pedro Porro and Nico Williams.The victory completed a remarkable campaign for Spain, who went unbeaten throughout the tournament and conceded only one goal in eight matches.Torres also made history by becoming only the fifth substitute to score in a FIFA World Cup final and the first since Germany's Mario Götze scored the winner against Argentina in the 2014 final.He also became just the second Barcelona player to score in a World Cup final after Andres Iniesta's extra-time winner for Spain in 2010.Nineteen-year-old Lamine Yamal lifted the World Cup in the biggest moment of his young career, just two years after helping Spain win UEFA Euro 2024.Spain's title-winning campaign was built on strong performances across the squad.Rodri controlled the midfield throughout the tournament, Mikel Oyarzabal finished with five goals to end among the leading scorers, while goalkeeper Unai Simon won the Golden Glove after a string of impressive displays.Spain started their campaign with a goalless draw against Cape Verde before beating Saudi Arabia 4-0 and Uruguay 1-0 to top Group H.They then defeated Austria 3-0 in the Round of 32, edged Portugal 1-0 in the Round of 16, beat Belgium 2-1 in the quarter-finals and overcame France 2-0 in the semi-finals.In the final, Spain dominated possession, created far more chances than Argentina and defeated the defending champions to seal another World Cup crown.
Article Summary: Spain beat Argentina 1-0 in the FIFA World Cup 2026 final in New York. Ferran Torres scored the only goal of the match in the 106th minute. Spain went unbeaten throughout the tournament and conceded only one goal. Head coach Lionel Scaloni said Lionel Messi would continue playing for as long as he wanted.

Article Title: Ferran Torres only the second player to achieve this rare FIFA World Cup final feat
Article Text:
Ferran Torres (PTI Photo)

Spain dominate but struggle to find breakthrough



Torres delivers the title-winning goal



NEW DELHI: Spain substitute Ferran Torres etched his name into FIFA World Cup history after scoring the winning goal in the 106th minute of the FIFA World Cup 2026 final against Argentina at the New York New Jersey Stadium on Sunday.With the strike, Torres became only the second substitute to score the winning goal in a World Cup final, matching Germany's Mario Götze, who also achieved the feat against Argentina in the 2014 final.Torres came off the bench in the 62nd minute and made the decisive impact as Spain defeated defending champions Argentina 1-0 after extra time to lift their second FIFA World Cup title. Spain's first World Cup triumph came in 2010.Spain controlled the final for long periods, enjoying most of the possession and creating several chances. They continued to push forward while Argentina, led by Lionel Messi , defended deep and managed to take the match into extra time despite being under constant pressure.By the time extra time began, Spain had already created far more chances but had been unable to beat Argentina goalkeeper Emiliano Martínez.The breakthrough finally arrived in the 106th minute. Nico Williams kept a long cross alive by heading the ball into the middle of the penalty area, where Torres reacted quickest.The Spanish forward struck a left-footed shot that flew under the crossbar and into the net, giving Martínez no chance.It was Spain's 20th shot of the match and Torres' first goal of the tournament. His goal proved enough to secure a famous 1-0 victory and Spain's second World Cup crown.Spain held on comfortably to end Argentina's reign as world champions and lift football's biggest trophy for the second time.
Article Summary: Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 after extra time to lift their second FIFA World Cup title. Ferran Torres became only the second substitute to score the winning goal in a World Cup final. Spain controlled the final for long periods, enjoying most of the possession and creating several chances.

Article Title: Has Lionel Messi ever won the FIFA World Cup Golden Boot? Full winners list and his World Cup record
Article Text:
Lionel Messi (Image Via Getty)

FIFA World Cup Golden Boot winners by year

World Cup Golden Boot winner Goals 2022 Kylian Mbappé 8 2018 Harry Kane 6 2014 James Rodríguez 6 2010 Thomas Müller 5 2006 Miroslav Klose 5 2002 Ronaldo 8 1998 Davor Šuker 6 1994 Oleg Salenko* 6 1994 Hristo Stoichkov* 6 1990 Salvatore Schillaci 6 1986 Gary Lineker 6 1982 Paolo Rossi 6 1978 Mario Kempes 6 1974 Grzegorz Lato 7 1970 Gerd Müller 10 1966 Eusébio 9 1962 Flórián Albert, Garrincha, Dražan Jerković, Leonel Sánchez, Valentin Ivanov and Vavá** 4 1958 Just Fontaine 13 1954 Sándor Kocsis 11 1950 Ademir 9 1938 Leônidas 7 1934 Oldřich Nejedlý 5 1930 Guillermo Stábile 8

How many FIFA World Cup Golden Boots does Lionel Messi have?

Lionel Messi’s World Cup goals and closest Golden Boot finish

Lionel Messi has won almost every major trophy in football, but there is one FIFA World Cup award that has always stayed out of his reach. Despite scoring 13 goals across five World Cups and leading Argentina to the 2022 title, Messi has never won the FIFA World Cup Golden Boot. His closest chance came in Qatar, where he scored seven goals but finished one behind Kylian Mbappé, who ended the tournament with eight.The FIFA World Cup Golden Boot is given to the player who scores the most goals during the tournament. The award has gone to some of football’s biggest names over the years, but Lionel Messi has never been on the winners list.*Shared award.**Six players finished as joint top scorers.Lionel Messi has never won the FIFA World Cup Golden Boot. His best chance came at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar. Messi scored seven goals and also provided three assists as Argentina lifted the trophy. However, Kylian Mbappé scored a hat-trick in the final against Argentina to finish the tournament with eight goals and claim the Golden Boot.Although Messi missed out on the top scorer award, he was named the FIFA World Cup Golden Ball winner as the tournament’s best player.It was the second time he received that honor after first winning it at the 2014 World Cup in Brazil.Messi finished his World Cup career with 13 goals, making him Argentina’s all-time top scorer in the tournament. He scored once in 2006, none in 2010, four in 2014, one in 2018 and seven in 2022.Even with that impressive record, he never finished as the tournament’s highest scorer. His seven goals in Qatar remain his closest attempt at winning the Golden Boot. Instead, that World Cup will always be remembered for helping him complete his career by lifting the one trophy he wanted most.
Article Summary: Lionel Messi has never won the FIFA World Cup Golden Boot. The award is given to the player who scores the most goals during the tournament. Messi has scored 13 goals in five World Cups. His closest chance came in Qatar, where he scored seven goals but finished one behind Kylian Mbappé who ended the tournament with eight.

Article Title: Jayden Adams' death takes a controversial turn as his girlfriend is dragged into the drama days after his tragic passing
Article Text:
Jayden Adams' death takes a controversial turn as his girlfriend is dragged into the drama days after his tragic passing.(Image via Getty Images)

Jayden Adams’ cause of death takes a controversial turn as his girlfriend gets dragged into the drama

South African midfielder and winger, Jayden Adams, passed away last weekend leaving millions of fans in mourning. While initially there were rumors of Jayden Adams committing suicide due to the grief he caried over his grandmother’s death last month, things have now taken a serious turn.In the last few hours, as per News24’s recent report, Jayden Adams reportedly had an argument with his girlfriend and the mother of his child, Aqueelah Adendorf, just hours before he tragically passed away.While it is not known what exactly happened between Jayden Adams and his long term girlfriend, Aqueelah Adendorf, it is being alleged that he committed suicide shortly after.Minutes later, Jayden Adams reportedly texted his father, “I can’t take it anymore”.However, these chain of events have not ben confirmed by South African authorities yet as Gayton McKenzie, minister ⁠of sports, arts and culture in South Africa, has brutaly slammed this report.A few hours ago, Gayton McKenzie, minister ⁠of sports, arts and culture in South Africa, took to X and wrote, “This is irresponsible reporting, do you want the young lady now to take her own life ? Do you want her child to blame her for the passing of his dad?”He added, “You are truly a tabloid, this country needs serious newspapers for sure.Let’s allow the police to do their job.”Last week, Gayton McKenzie had also paid a tribute to Jayden Adams in an emotional statement as he wrote, “I shall carry forever the humble, ⁠appreciative response he gave me. That he chose to wear the national jersey and give his all for his country in that moment speaks to a depth of character and professionalism well beyond his years, ‌and it reflects the calibre of young man South Africa has lost…”However, neither Jayden Adams nor his family has responded t the speculations yet as the family tries to mourn his death in private.Interestingly, Jayden Adams’ girlfriend and the mother of his child, Aqueelah Adendorf, has also remained distant from the speculations as she posted a heartfelt tribute last week.Jayden Adams’ girlfriend, Aqueelah Adendorf, wrote, “There are no words to describe the pain I’m feeling. “Rest in peace, my love.”She added, “Thank you for every memory, every laugh, every hug, and every moment we shared. You were not only the love of my life but also my greatest supporter and my best friend.”At this point in time, it has not been confirmed how the 25 year old South African midfielder and winger, Jayden Adams, passed away last weekend as authorities remain involved in the high profile investigation.
Article Summary: Jayden Adams' cause of death takes a controversial turn as his girlfriend gets dragged into the drama. South African midfielder and winger, Jayden Adams, passed away last weekend leaving millions of fans in mourning. It is being alleged that he committed suicide shortly after an argument with his long term girlfriend, Aqueelah Adendorf.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Confucius: 'If I am walking with two other men, each of them will serve as my teacher...' and the lesson of finding wisdom in everyone around you
Article Text:
'If I am walking with two other men, each of them will serve as my teacher'

philosopher

Confucius

“If I am walking with two other men, each of them will serve as my teacher.

I will pick out the good points of the one and imitate them, and the bad points of the other and correct them in myself.”

The historic road to self-cultivation

Kong Fuzi

"When three people walk together, one of them must be my teacher."

The dual mirror of human nature

Radical humility in the modern era

Finding teachers in everyday life

Imagine three people walking along a dusty road in fifth-century BC China. One walks with good posture, taking calm, respectful steps. The second keeps stumbling, complaining loudly, and pushing past others in frustration. The third person, quietly watching both companions, is the. Instead of seeing the second man as simply annoying or praising the first as naturally better, Confucius treats both as valuable teachers. He chooses to copy the dignity of the first man and uses the rude behaviour of the second as a chance to examine his own hidden weaknesses.This idea is at the centre of one of the best-known passages in the Analects:At its heart, this teaching removes the traditional and strict idea of who can be called a teacher. It suggests that wisdom is not something found only in famous schools or held only by respected masters. Instead, learning becomes an ongoing and active choice. The quote explains that every human interaction, no matter how ordinary or frustrating, can become a chance for personal growth if a person has the right mindset.It continues to remain relevant because it speaks to a common human habit: judging other people instead of using their actions to improve ourselves.This idea comes directly from, better known around the world as Confucius, who lived during China's Spring and Autumn period between 770 BC and 476 BC. It was a time marked by the gradual decline of the Zhou Dynasty, political corruption, constant wars, and social unrest. In a divided society where rulers depended on force to control their people, Confucius travelled from one state to another, trying to persuade leaders to return to ethical government, proper rituals, and moral responsibility.These words were collected after his death by his loyal students in Book 7 of the Analects, a collection of sayings and conversations that became the foundation of Confucian philosophy. The original Classical Chinese phrase, San ren xing, bi you wo shi yan, literally means,Confucius was speaking to his students, young men preparing to become future government officials and advisers to kings.By presenting himself as someone who was always learning, Confucius taught an important lesson in intellectual humility. Although he was considered the most educated thinker of his time, he rejected the idea of being an all-knowing sage. Instead, he described himself as an ordinary traveller who stayed open to learning from everyone he met, creating an example his students were expected to follow throughout their public lives.From a philosophical point of view, this teaching supports the central Confucian idea of xiushen, or the careful improvement of one's own character. In the wider tradition of virtue ethics, becoming a good person is not about memorising fixed rules but about practising good behaviour every day. The image of walking beside two different companions creates two mirrors for understanding human nature. The first mirror encourages imitation, where seeing someone else's strengths inspires us to develop the same qualities. The second mirror, which reflects bad behaviour, may be even more important. Instead of creating pride or encouraging criticism, the faults of others become an early warning that helps us recognise similar weaknesses within ourselves.This idea also connects with modern psychology, especially the concept of psychological projection. People are often very good at noticing faults in others while failing to see those same faults in themselves. Confucius turned this habit around. By using another person's irritating behaviour as a reason to examine himself, he changed everyday conflict into a chance for personal understanding.This principle also quietly challenged the strict social and intellectual rankings of ancient China. It suggested that a farmer, a merchant, or even an aggressive soldier could teach just as much about good character as an experienced court scholar. The responsibility for learning rested completely with the student rather than the teacher, making wisdom available to anyone willing to observe others with humility.Applying this ancient teaching in 2026 offers a powerful answer to today's echo chambers and defensive attitudes. In modern workplaces, public life, and online discussions, it is easy to dismiss people who behave badly or disagree with us. Real leadership means looking beyond frustration and finding useful lessons even in difficult situations.In today's workplace psychology, this idea appears through active feedback and self-reflection. When a team leader works with an unreliable or poor communicator, the normal reaction is frustration. Someone following the Confucian approach instead treats that person's poor communication as a clear example of what to avoid, making sure to give better and clearer updates to their own team. In the same way, watching a colleague stay calm during a major crisis provides a practical example worth copying.The same philosophy can even be seen in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning systems. Engineers train models using datasets that contain mistakes, bias, and other problems. Instead of throwing away all the faulty data, they study those failures to build stronger safeguards into future systems, allowing the software to improve by learning from earlier mistakes.The greatest strength of this philosophy is that anyone can practise it. It does not require university degrees, professional qualifications, or expensive training courses. It only asks people to make a conscious choice to see the world around them as a living classroom.Every workplace, bus, train, and family gathering is full of possible teachers. The rude passenger on the morning commute becomes a lesson in patience and self-control, while the colleague who listens carefully during a stressful meeting becomes an example of professional respect. By treating everyday interactions as opportunities to build character, personal growth stops being an abstract idea and becomes part of daily life.Historical records show that Confucius spent his final years editing ancient texts and teaching around 3,000 students, with 72 of them later becoming respected philosophers themselves. His influence lasted through centuries of political change because his advice remained closely connected to ordinary human experience. The true measure of wisdom is not found in living alone, but in how well a person learns from the people walking beside them.
Article Summary: Confucius said: 'If I am walking with two other men, each of them will serve as my teacher' The idea is at the centre of one of the best-known passages in the Analects. It suggests that wisdom is not something found only in famous schools. Instead, learning becomes an ongoing and active choice.

Article Title: Late drama! Enzo Fernandez sent off as Argentina reduced to 10 men before World Cup final extra time
Article Text:
Referee Slavko Vincic shows a red card to Argentina's Enzo Fernandez (24) during the World Cup final. (AP Photo)

Spain dominate but Martinez keeps Argentina alive



Historic goalless final reaches tense extra time



The FIFA World Cup 2026 final produced late drama before extra time as Argentina were forced to play the additional 30 minutes with 10 men after Enzo Fernandez was sent off in stoppage time against Spain.With the score locked at 0-0 after 90 minutes, Fernandez received a second yellow card in the 93rd minute following a late challenge on Spain defender Pau Cubarsi, leaving Lionel Messi 's side a man down at the most crucial stage of the title clash.The incident came moments after Argentina had survived another Spain attack. The ball had been cleared back upfield following an Emiliano Martinez save, but as Argentina attempted to launch their own move forward, Fernandez arrived late and caught Cubarsi with a reckless challenge.The referee had no hesitation in showing the midfielder a second yellow card, despite Fernandez's protests.Argentina were forced to defend the closing moments of normal time with 10 players and entered extra time facing an even greater challenge against a Spain side that had dominated possession throughout the match.Spain controlled the contest for large periods, producing a commanding display against the defending champions.La Roja finished regulation time with 15 shots compared to Argentina's zero, nine corners to Argentina's one, and overwhelming possession. Yet they were unable to find the breakthrough against an inspired Emiliano Martinez.The Argentina goalkeeper produced a record-breaking performance in the final, making 10 saves — the most ever in a World Cup final — to keep his team alive.One of his biggest interventions came immediately after Fernandez's dismissal when Spain teenager Lamine Yamal stepped up for a dangerous free-kick from around 22 yards. The 19-year-old's curling effort looked destined for the corner, but Martinez produced a stunning diving save to deny him.The goalkeeper immediately sprang to his feet, punching the air and urging the crowd to raise the noise inside the stadium.The deadlock after 90 minutes added another chapter to World Cup final history.The match was the ninth World Cup final to remain goalless at halftime, joining previous classics such as Spain vs Netherlands in 2010 and Germany vs Argentina in 2014.The opening half had already created a unique record, with just three total shots — the fewest in the first half of a World Cup final since records began in 1966.Argentina had also become the first team since 1966 to fail to register a first-half shot in any World Cup match.While Spain continued their push for a second World Cup title after their 2010 triumph, Argentina were chasing a fourth crown and hoping to become the first nation since Brazil in 1962 to successfully defend the trophy.
Article Summary: Enzo Fernandez sent off in 93rd minute for late challenge on Pau Cubarsi. Argentina forced to play final 30 minutes with 10 men after Fernandez's dismissal. Match was the ninth World Cup final to remain goalless at halftime. Argentina goalkeeper Emiliano Martinez made 10 saves to keep his team alive.

Article Title: FIFA WC final: Referee's accidental spray leaves Spain's Alex Baena wearing shaving cream in hilarious final blooper
Article Text:
Foam sprayed on Alex Baena (Photo/FIFA /X)

A Fresh Coat of Paint

Fans in Stitches

High-stakes World Cup finals are usually defined by tension, brilliant skill, and dramatic refereeing decisions. However, Sunday's grand finale between Spain and Argentina at MetLife Stadium provided a moment of pure, unscripted comedy that instantly broke the internet.Spanish midfielder Álex Baena found himself on the receiving end of a bizarre field mishap when match referee Slavko Vinčić accidentally blasted him square in the face with a generous dollop of vanishing spray.The hilarious incident unfolded in the heat of a tactical stoppage. As Vinčić reached down to mark out the ten-yard line for a free-kick wall, the Slovenian official misjudged his grip on the pressurized canister. Instead of painting a neat white line across the turf, the nozzle popped in the wrong direction, launching a thick cloud of white foam straight into Baena’s face and torso.For a brief second, the 24-year-old Spanish international stood frozen, looking like he had just signed up for a mid-match shaving cream commercial.The hilarious clip quickly exploded across social media platform X, racking up millions of views within minutes as global fans replayed the moment in slow motion.Commentators couldn't contain their giggles either, remarking that while referees usually issue yellow cards to cool down players, Vinčić found a far more literal way to frost the Spanish squad.Fortunately, both men handled the awkward moment with good humor. A visibly embarrassed Vinčić offered an immediate apology, helping the midfielder wipe off the white suds. Baena cracked a wide smile, wiped his eyes clean with his jersey, and quickly scampered back into position to resume the action, completely unharmed, albeit freshly white-washed for the global audience!
Article Summary: Referee Slavko Vinčić accidentally sprayed Spanish midfielder Alex Baena with vanishing spray. The incident occurred during Sunday's World Cup final between Spain and Argentina at MetLife Stadium. The clip quickly exploded across social media platform X, racking up millions of views within minutes.

Article Title: IShowSpeed’s wild reaction to BTS steals attention during FIFA World Cup Final halftime show
Article Text:
IShowSpeed’s wild reaction to BTS steals attention during FIFA World Cup Final halftime show (Image via Getty)

IShowSpeed goes all out as BTS perform ‘Dynamite’ at FIFA World Cup Final

Why IShowSpeed’s BTS reaction meant so much to ARMYs?

The first-ever FIFA World Cup Final halftime show created plenty of memorable moments, but one unexpected reaction grabbed almost as much attention as the performance itself. Popular streamer IShowSpeed, whose real name is Darren Watkins Jr. , was caught on camera celebrating wildly as BTS appeared on stage during the Spain vs. Argentina final at MetLife Stadium on July 19, 2026. A fan account on X, @solmerv, shared the clip with the caption, “SPEED’S REACTION TO BTS’ PERFORMANCE HELP 😭 #BTSxWorldCup2026.” The short video spread across social media, with many fans enjoying the crossover between one of the world’s biggest streamers and one of the world’s biggest music groups.The video, which is about 15 seconds long, shows Speed sitting in the stadium stands before suddenly jumping to his feet as BTS appear on the giant screens. He points excitedly toward the stage, dances with full energy, throws his arms around, shouts with his mouth wide open and looks completely caught up in the moment.People sitting around him can also be seen watching the performance. Some smile at his reaction, while others appear to record the moment on their phones.The packed stadium, giant screens and halftime visuals can all be seen in the background.BTS performed “Dynamite” wearing matching black-and-red outfits during the star-filled halftime show. The performance also featured artists including Madonna, Justin Bieber, Shakira and Burna Boy, making it one of the biggest entertainment events of the tournament.The reaction did not surprise many fans who have followed Speed recently. During one of his live streams before the World Cup Final, he openly said that BTS is his favourite K-pop group, while also mentioning that his brother prefers Stray Kids. Because of that, many BTS fans were happy to see his genuine excitement when the group took the stage.Speed is one of the world’s most popular online creators, known for his loud and energetic football streams, gaming videos and entertaining reactions. He has spent much of the 2026 FIFA World Cup attending matches in person and sharing his experiences with millions of viewers. He also released his own World Cup-themed song, “Champions,” during the tournament.The halftime show itself also carried special meaning. It was the first halftime show in FIFA World Cup Final history, bringing a Super Bowl-style music performance to football’s biggest match. For BTS, it was another major global appearance following the group’s reunion after military service and during their Arirang world tour.Speed’s reaction added an unexpected highlight to an already historic night, giving fans of football, streaming and K-pop a fun moment that quickly became one of the most talked-about clips from the final.
Article Summary: Streamer IShowSpeed was caught on camera celebrating wildly as BTS appeared on stage during the FIFA World Cup Final. The short video spread across social media, with many fans enjoying the crossover. BTS performed ‘Dynamite’ wearing matching black-and-red outfits during the star-filled halftime show.
